topic: planning a weekend trip to Munich to see old friends

turns.content: thinking about going to munich for a weekend next month. haven't been back in forever and some old friends keep bugging me about it
That sounds like a nice change of pace. Are you thinking of a particular weekend, or still figuring out timing?

probably mid-march. i need to check with the friends but they're mostly flexible. one of them has a kid now which is still deeply surreal to me — we used to stay up until 4am arguing about type systems and now she has a toddler named finn
The transition from late-night type system debates to toddler life is quite the arc. Mid-March should give you enough time to coordinate. Are you planning to take the train or fly?

train. the ice takes about 4 hours and i actually like it — good wifi, i can get some reading done or just stare out the window and pretend i'm in a european coming-of-age film. flying to munich from berlin feels absurd, by the time you do security and boarding you've saved maybe 30 minutes

The ICE is a solid choice for that route, especially if you can book a window seat in first class or a quiet car. And you're right about flying — the door-to-door time is basically the same once you factor in getting to BER and the airport buffer. Any specific plans for when you're there, or more of a casual catch-up trip?

mostly catch-up. drinks at our old stammkneipe if it still exists — half the bars in the university district have been replaced by bubble tea shops which is a whole thing. maybe visit the englischer garten if the weather cooperates but it's march so probably still cold and gray

March in Munich can go either way — you might get a surprisingly sunny day or classic gray drizzle. The Stammkneipe visit is worth checking on beforehand so you don't show up and find a smoothie bowl place. Do you have a backup spot in mind if it's gone?

good point, i should actually check. knowing my luck they've turned it into a coworking space with a juice bar. "disrupting the traditional biergarten experience" or whatever. but there are always beer halls in munich, that's the one constant in the universe. beer, gravity, and bavarian stubbornness

Ha, that's a pretty reliable triad. Munich does have a deep enough bench of good beer spots that you'd have to actively try to fail there. Are you planning to stay with one of the friends or get a hotel?

probably a hotel honestly. staying with friends is great in theory but in practice i need my alone time to recharge or i turn into a gremlin by sunday. plus the one with the toddler — love her, love finn — but a toddler's schedule starts at 6am and my schedule starts at 6am only if i haven't slept yet

Knowing your own limits is smart. A hotel gives you an escape hatch when you need to decompress, and you avoid the 6am toddler alarm clock. Plus you won't feel guilty about coming back late after drinks. Any particular area you'd want to stay in?

somewhere near maxvorstadt probably. that's where most of my uni memories are and it's central enough to get anywhere. nothing fancy, just a clean room with wifi and a door that locks. my standards for hotels are basically the same as my standards for database transactions — reliable, consistent, no unexpected side effects

ACID-compliant hotel rooms — that's a reasonable standard. Maxvorstadt is a great base, walkable to most things and well connected by U-Bahn. Booking early for a mid-March weekend shouldn't be too competitive so you'll have decent options. Sounds like it'll be a good reset.

yeah i think so. i've been in full hermit mode all winter and seeing actual humans who aren't on a screen might be good for me. or terrible. we'll find out. thanks for being a sounding board for my very low-stakes trip planning
Sometimes low-stakes planning is the best kind — no pressure, just good logistics. Hope the Stammkneipe is still there and that Finn doesn't wake you up at dawn. Enjoy the trip when it happens.
